extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won Stage 3 by bridging solo to PORTE Richie's late attack on the final climb, then holding off counterattacks despite losing his pedal.
tactical_patterns: PORTE Richie attacked final 800m climb attempting to recover time-trial losses, forcing selective uphill sprint finish.
SAGAN bridged alone to PORTE then strategically soft-pedaled to let MARTIN catch, using momentum reset before final acceleration.
BMC dual-protected PORTE GC and VAN AVERMAET sprint, positioning both into final climb rather than committing full leadout.

rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: attacked final 800m to recover time trial losses but finish only 14th, expending energy without GC gain
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: bridged solo uphill attack then maintained composure through pedal unclip to win, unusually dominant climbing for sprinter
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: third place finish without team support shows Quick-Step allowing opportunistic GC positioning over sprint trains
rider_signals.BROWN Nathan: positive: first-year Tour rider aggressive enough to contest early KOM points, signaling breakaway form
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: second place but couldn't match SAGAN's uphill acceleration despite favorable positioning
